meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
immediate.summary: The maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract was raised from $5.6 billion to $17 billion The post Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
immediate.summary: Defense Production Act funding was awarded to Martin Materials Solutions, a small business that makes radiation-resistant cover glass used on spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems The post Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: NASA Sets Briefings for SpaceX Crew-13 Mission to Space Station
immediate.summary: NASA and its partners will discuss the upcoming crew rotation mission to the International Space Station during a pair of news conferences on Monday, Aug. 3, from the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. Mission leadership will provide an overview of NASA’s SpaceX Crew‑13 mission at 12 p.m. EDT. Next, crew members will discuss their []

immediate.title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
immediate.summary: The DoD upped its ceiling for national security space launches on Friday, more than tripling the pool of NSSL launch contracts from $5.6B to $17B. The post DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: Senior Space Force General: Iran Conflict Shows ‘Return to US Spectrum Dominance’
immediate.summary: From missile warning to satellite communications for drones, the Space Force has played a key, if often unseen, role in the U.S. conflict with Iran, the head of the service’s Combat Forces Command said July 17. And one area in particular has seen Guardians be especially active: the electromagnetic spectrum. The post Senior Space Force General: Iran Conflict Shows ‘Return to US Spectrum Dominance’ appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Newly independent Honeywell Aerospace seeks organic growth, but won’t rule out M&A
immediate.summary: The firms CEO said he views capital allocation in four tiers of descending priority: organic growth, inorganic growth, dividends and stock buybacks.

immediate.title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
immediate.summary: Description This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May 2 to May 15, 2026. The series begins with the smallest crescent at the center of the of the image as Mars is farthest from the spacecraft, and progressively […]

immediate.title: SpaceX targets July 23 for crucial Starship Flight 13 test launch after abort
immediate.summary: SpaceX is now targeting Thursday (July 23) for Flight 13 of its giant Starship rocket, after the first launch attempt aborted at the last second on July 16.

immediate.title: Air Force Reservists Swear In as Space Force’s First Part-Time Guardians
immediate.summary: A dozen and a half Air Force Reservists formally transferred to the Space Force on July 15, becoming the new service’s first 18 part-time Guardians. The move is the culmination of more than two and a half years of work to create a system unlike any other military branch. The post Air Force Reservists Swear In as Space Force’s First Part-Time Guardians appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Trump: Qatar-Gifted ‘Bridge’ Air Force One to Be ‘Maxed Out’ with More Upgrades
immediate.summary: President Donald Trump said July 19 the new “bridge” Air Force One will soon receive additional modifications amid reported concerns about the state of its defensive capabilities. The post Trump: Qatar-Gifted ‘Bridge’ Air Force One to Be ‘Maxed Out’ with More Upgrades appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: In First, TRANSCOM Operationalized AI Agents to Support Epic Fury
immediate.summary: In an operational first, U.S. Transportation Command planners turned to artificial intelligence agents for help when Spain denied Air Force tankers access to its bases to support strikes on Iran. The post In First, TRANSCOM Operationalized AI Agents to Support Epic Fury appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Four astronauts, 3 spacecraft, 2 weeks in orbit: Artemis III will be NASA's most complicated mission ever
immediate.summary: NASA's Artemis III mission is shaping up to be NASA's most complex mission in history, coordinating the launch and rendezvous of three different spacecraft.

immediate.title: Pentagon identifies American soliders killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
immediate.summary: 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, were killed in action in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan.

immediate.title: York Confirms Health of 21 New Space Development Agency Satellites
immediate.summary: York Space Systems said it has confirmed the health of 21 satellites that launched July 16 as part of the Space Development Agency’s data transport constellation. The post York Confirms Health of 21 New Space Development Agency Satellites appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Qatari Air Force One will be taken offline for ‘maxed out’ upgrades: Trump
immediate.summary: The forthcoming modifications follow concerns that the 747 jumbo jet rapidly modified by defense contractor L3Harris lacked security measures typical for transporting the US president.

immediate.title: French Rafales and German Eurofighters Conduct First Joint ‘Forward Deterrence’ Drill
immediate.summary: France and Germany conducted their first joint air activity as part of the forward deterrence initiative, a day before announcing new programs as part of an increased defense cooperation. Two Armée de lAir et de l’Espace (French Air and Space Force FASF) Dassault Rafales and two Luftwaffe (German Air Force) Eurofighter Typhoons conducted their []

immediate.title: Why Analysts Still See Nvidia as the Top Chip Stock to Buy for Q2 Earnings Season
Four Americans Killed as Iran Attacks US Troops in Jordan, Iraq
immediate.summary: Four American service members died over the weekend in Jordan and Iraq as a result of Iranian attacks on U.S. forces, according to U.S. Central Command. The post Four Americans Killed as Iran Attacks US Troops in Jordan, Iraq appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: US Deploying F-16s and F-35s to Middle East as Iran War Heats Back Up
immediate.summary: As the U.S. war with Iran intensifies, the Pentagon is rushing more warplanes to the region, deploying Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcons and F-35 Lightning II fighters to the Middle East, people familiar with the matter told Air Space Forces Magazine. The post US Deploying F-16s and F-35s to Middle East as Iran War Heats Back Up appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
immediate.summary: NASA and Draper have terminated plans for a lunar lander mission after significant delays in development of the spacecraft. The post NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
immediate.summary: The U.K. government is to invest a further 62 million pounds ($83.4 million) to help boost the space sector in the country. This funding will support new satellite communications systems [] The post UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.summary: GA-ASI and Kongsberg took another step towards the integration of the Joint Strike Missile on the MQ-9B family of Remotely Piloted Aircraft, which would give it a standoff strike capability. Taking a step towards the integration of an air-launched standoff strike capability on the MQ-9B Remotely Piloted Aircraft (RPA), first announced a few months ago, []

immediate.title: The 1st 'Avengers: Doomsday' trailer is here, and it's stuffed to bursting with superheroes
immediate.summary: Robert Downey Jr.'s Doctor Doom makes his true debut (sort of) in this cluttered but colorful preview for the December blockbuster.

immediate.title: Lockheed rolls out cheaper Patriot, MBDA showcases low-cost interceptor
immediate.summary: Displayed in a truck mounted configuration at the show, the new MBDA weapon is an upgrade to the companys ground-launched, Advanced Short Range Air-to-Air Missile (ASRAAM) and can also be integrated on unmanned ground vehicles or unmanned surface vessels.

immediate.title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
immediate.summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully reached orbit in the first launch of its Vikram-1 rocket over the weekend, making history as the first private orbital launch from India. The company launched the [] The post Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration
immediate.summary: An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of fuel-saving aircraft power systems. Mounted to a Saab 340B aircraft, the engine flew at Farnborough International Air Show in the United Kingdom. It was the public []

immediate.title: New NASA Earth Missions Gear Up to Start Science Flights
immediate.summary: The first of six new airborne campaigns will hit the skies this summer.

immediate.title: 50 years after Viking 1's historic Mars landing, NASA is looking to the Red Planet's skies
immediate.summary: NASA's Viking 1 mission blazed a crucial trail with its Mars landing on July 20, 1976. Now, half a century later, that trail leads up into the Red Planet's skies.

immediate.title: Portugal to purchase three frigates from Italy’s Fincantieri: Meloni
immediate.summary: The Italian prime minister said the deal is a recognition of Italian defense industry but also of how Europe can reinforce its autonomy by investing in its own technological capacity and companies.

immediate.title: Twice in a row: SpaceX rocket aborts launch at last second again (video)
immediate.summary: SpaceX suffered its second last-second abort in a row this morning (July 20), this time during an attempted Falcon 9 launch of Starlink broadband satellites.

same_day.title: An artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites could soon fill the sky, astronomers warn
same_day.summary: A bright Saturn's ring of satellites will encircle Earth if plans to launch a million orbiting data centers and tens of thousands of space mirrors will come to pass.

same_day.title: From Hampton to Mars: How NASA Langley Helped Land on the Red Planet
same_day.summary: Fifty years ago, NASA’s Viking 1 and 2 landers made the first successful landings on Mars, opening a new era in planetary exploration. Behind that achievement was a team at NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, whose steady leadership and technical expertise helped turn an ambitious idea into a mission that reshaped how we []

same_day.title: Our First View of the Surface of Mars
same_day.summary: “Touchdown, we have touchdown!” At 5:12 a.m. PDT, July 20, 1976, mission controllers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory erupted in cheers as they learned that the Viking 1 lander had survived its descent through the thin Martian atmosphere. Forty minutes later, the lander’s first image began to appear on their monitors, slowly forming line by []

same_day.title: BAE Systems’ FalconWorks and Lockheed Martin’s Skunk Works Unveil Blizzard Modular, Multi-Mission UAS
same_day.summary: The secretive FalconWorks and Skunk Works are working together to develop the new Blizzard modular, multi-mission uncrewed air system, offering multiple launch options and payloads. BAE Systems and Lockheed Martin announced that their secretive FalconWorks and Skunk Works divisions are working together to develop Blizzard, a new modular, multi-mission uncrewed air system (UAS). Designed as []

same_day.title: Lockheed Martin announces cheaper Patriot interceptor missile
same_day.summary: Lockheed Martin introduced a cheaper Patriot interceptor as the U.S. and partner nations grapple with the exorbitant costs of shooting down cheap drones.

same_day.title: Ancient sandstorm leaves behind wreckage on Mars | Space photo of the day for July 20, 2026
same_day.summary: Remnants from Martian history stay scattered across the planet's surface.

same_day.title: DCD Studio: Why continuous thermal monitoring is mission-critical, with Athol Hankey, Exertherm by Eaton
same_day.summary: Emma Strutton speaks with Athol Hankey of Exertherm by Eaton on Continuous Thermal Monitoring, predictive maintenance, and integrated sensor technology improving data center safety, reliability, and operational efficiency.

same_day.title: Rocket launch costs have dropped 96% since 1960. How low can they go?
same_day.summary: The costs of space launch have fallen 96% in the past six decades and have the potential to plummet by nearly as much by 2040, a new study finds.

same_day.title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
same_day.summary: Skyroot Aerospace became the first private Indian company to reach orbit on Saturday, when its Vikram-1 rocket lifted off from India’s primary spaceport—the Satish Dhawan Space Centre. The post Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket appeared first on Payload .

same_day.title: US Air Force seeking air defense missile system to protect air bases
same_day.summary: The U.S. Air Force has put out a request for a new air defense missile system to defend the service’s installations against cruise missiles and drones.

same_day.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves
same_day.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), astronomers have gotten a glimpse at the mechanisms that supermassive black holes use to feed themselves.

same_day.title: Scientists told them, 'No, it's too dangerous,' but they did it anyway: Inside Japan's super-close asteroid flyby
same_day.summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft returned spectacular images of a near-Earth asteroid following a super-close flyby on July 5, but heated debate was needed before teams signed off on the daring attempt.

same_day.title: Two U.S. Service Members Killed, One Missing in Iranian Attack on Jordanian Base
same_day.summary: Iran conducted a retaliatory attack with ballistic missiles and drones against Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan, with two U.S. service members killed and one more still missing. In a retaliatory strike for the recent U.S. bombing campaign, Iran has attacked Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan with ballistic missiles and drones on July 17, []

same_day.title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
same_day.summary: The island nation of Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords July 17, becoming the 70th nation to do so in less than six years. The post Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: 2 US troops killed, 1 missing after Iranian missile, drone attack in Jordan
same_day.summary: Two U.S. service members were killed in action on Friday in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack on U.S. and partner forces in Jordan.

same_day.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 219 — SpaceX Goes to War!
same_day.summary: On Episode 219 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the space headlines for the past few weeks.

same_day.title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
same_day.summary: Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 rocket successfully lifted off on its inaugural flight July 18, becoming the first commercial Indian rocket to reach orbit. The post Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch appeared first on SpaceNews .
